scope.summary: Commits the C1 wire-contract fixture: an OAuth bearer JWT and HS256 secret minted once out of tree with PyJWT, asserted through the real Hs256Verifier and the real HTTP OAuth gate, with four negatives and a committed-red diagnostics test that bead oraclemcp-091-b2-oauth-contract-g5xmr flips green.
out_of_scope: Any change to the OAuth verifier, the HTTP challenge builder, or documentation — B2a/B2b (oraclemcp-091-b2-oauth-contract-g5xmr) owns the fix, this bead owns only the fixture.
Making the four negative rejection classes distinguishable; that assertion is committed in an explicitly-marked expected-failure state.
Asymmetric (RS256/ES256/JWKS) token verification, which remains a fail-closed seam with no shipped verifier.

entry_points.name: POST /mcp with an Authorization: Bearer header, OAuth enforcement configured
entry_points.trace: c1_externally_minted_token_authorizes_a_real_mcp_request drives handle_http_request end to end and asserts 200 plus a JSON-RPC initialize result; c1_negatives_are_refused_with_an_rfc6750_challenge asserts 401, the RFC 6750 error code, and that no token material is echoed back

entry_points.name: ResourceServerConfig::validate under the production Hs256Verifier
entry_points.trace: c1_externally_minted_token_is_accepted_by_the_production_verifier and c1_hmac_key_is_the_raw_utf8_secret_not_its_base64_decoding pin acceptance and the raw-UTF-8 key contract in both directions

limitations: Scoped test only: cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --test c1_oauth_literal_jwt (5 passed, 1 expected-failure) plus the deliberate red run of that expected failure. The full workspace gate was not run for this commit.

The pairwise-distinct-diagnostics assertion is committed red by design and does not execute in the default run. It is not cited as proof of anything here; its recorded red output is the deliverable, and oraclemcp-091-b2-oauth-contract-g5xmr flips it green by deleting the #[ignore] with the assertions unchanged.
The fixture covers HS256 only. RS256/ES256 tokens route through the SignatureVerifier seam, which ships no implementation, so no external mint could be exercised for them.
The token and secret constants cannot be regenerated in-repo without recreating the self-reference the fixture removes; changing the claims means minting again with an external tool.
follow_ups: oraclemcp-091-b2-oauth-contract-g5xmr must delete the #[ignore] on c1_negative_tokens_are_pairwise_distinguishable_on_the_wire as part of its acceptance.
